synthesized_reason: Phase A pass; correctness + test-coverage pass (60/60 tests, including round-trip and schema-invalid). Legitimate medium finding: generate-reintegration uses inline validation instead of the canonical schema. Follow-up row should swap it. Not a blocker — generator output still round-trips through schema-validated get-reintegration-json.

real_findings.note: generate-reintegration.sh uses jq-based subset validation (lines 357-400) rather than the official schemas/reintegration.schema.json via Python jsonschema. get-reintegration-json was fixed to use the schema (HIGH-2, 9576e0a), but generate still uses the inline subset. Schema is authoritative — generate should too. Follow-up: swap generate validation to use the same Draft2020-12Validator pattern.
schemas/reintegration.schema.json does not declare test_results.evidence_path as required; the AC-R2 contract implies it is. Follow-up: tighten schema.
SC2038 in generate-reintegration.sh; SC3001 process substitution in test-reintegration.sh under #!/bin/sh shebang (bash-ism under sh declaration). Minor.

false_positives: unplanned-changes: "file_ownership omits generate-reintegration.sh" — plan.json wave-2 lists generate-reintegration.sh under this deliverable's ownership. The definition.yaml may not enumerate every sub-file but plan.json is the binding ownership contract.

reviewers.phase_b_fresh_claude: skipped: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY absent; codex-only satisfies generator-evaluator separation via cross-model
